data.narrative:
AQT is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Rainer Blatt, Thomas Monz, and Peter Zoller. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 identified peers: 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private firms, with notable private peers including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to AQT at this stage. The quantum computing peer set, while containing 7 same-sector comparables, lacks sufficient depth within narrowly-defined filters: zero companies share both AQT's Series A stage and comparable valuation tier, only one peer occupies an adjacent stage, and the private market lacks three or more sufficiently similar datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models, maturity levels, and technical approaches across the available peer universe.

Without a statistically adequate peer cohort, standard triangulation methods cannot be reliably applied. A credible valuation of AQT would require alternative approaches—such as venture capital method analysis using explicit assumptions on exit multiples and time horizon, or detailed technical and commercial due diligence anchoring to development milestones and market adoption rates. The absence of disclosed raise amount and current valuation further constrains quantitative assessment. Additional data on AQT's revenue, customer traction, or technical IP differentiation would be necessary to establish a defensible valuation range.
